name: PHASE 1: THE BEDROCK (Syntax, Keywords, Core Libs)
description:
# Phase 1: The Bedrock

This phase covers the fundamental building blocks of Java. You will learn about the environment, the compilation process, strict keywords, identifiers, primitive types, and control flow.

subTopics.name: 1.1 Environment & Toolchain
subTopics.description:
# Environment & Toolchain

Java operates on the **Write Once, Run Anywhere** (WORA) principle.

## 1. JDK (Java Development Kit)
The full kit required to *develop* Java applications.
- **`javac`**: The compiler that turns `.java` source files into `.class` bytecode.
- **`jar`**: Archiver tool to package multiple files into a single `.jar` archive.
- **`javadoc`**: Generator for HTML documentation from source code comments.

## 2. JRE (Java Runtime Environment)
The environment required to *run* Java applications.
- **JVM**: The Virtual Machine.
- **Core Libraries**: `rt.jar`, containing standard classes like `String`, `Math`, etc.

## 3. JVM (Java Virtual Machine)
The engine that executes bytecode.
- **Interpreter**: Executes bytecode instruction by instruction.
- **JIT (Just-In-Time) Compiler**: Translates frequently executed bytecode (hotspots) into native machine code at runtime for performance.

## 4. Compilation Lifecycle
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Source File .java] -->|javac| B[Bytecode File .class]
    B -->|java Launcher| C[JVM Execution]
    C -->|JIT/Interpreter| D[Machine Code]
```

subTopics.name: 1.2 Keywords & Identifiers
subTopics.description:
# Keywords & Identifiers

Java has over 50 strict reserved keywords that cannot be used as variable names.

## Access Modifiers
| Modifier | Class | Package | Subclass | World |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `public` | âś… | âś… | âś… | âś… |
| `protected` | âś… | âś… | âś… | âťŚ |
| (default) | âś… | âś… | âťŚ | âťŚ |
| `private` | âś… | âťŚ | âťŚ | âťŚ |

## Core Keywords
- **`class`, `interface`, `enum`, `record`**: Object definitions.
- **`new`**: Allocates memory on the Heap.
- **`this`**: Reference to the current instance.
- **`super`**: Reference to the parent instance.
- **`static`**: Belongs to the class, not the instance.
- **`final`**: Cannot be modified, overridden, or extended.

## Naming Rules
- Must start with a letter, `_`, or `$`. Cannot start with a digit.
- **Conventions:**
  - `camelCase`: methods, variables.
  - `PascalCase`: Classes, Interfaces.
  - `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`: Constants.

subTopics.name: 1.3 Primitive Data Types
subTopics.description:
# Primitive Data Types

Java has 8 primitive data types stored directly on the Stack.

## Integer Family
- `byte` (8-bit): -128 to 127
- `short` (16-bit): -32,768 to 32,767
- `int` (32-bit): Default for integers.
- `long` (64-bit): Requires `L` suffix (e.g., `100L`).

## Floating Family
- `float` (32-bit): Requires `f` suffix (e.g., `3.14f`).
- `double` (64-bit): Default for decimals.

## Others
- `char` (16-bit): Unsigned Unicode, single quotes (`'A'`).
- `boolean` (1-bit): `true` or `false`.

## Type Conversion (Casting)
```java
// Widening (Implicit)
int a = 100;
long b = a;

// Narrowing (Explicit Cast)
double pi = 3.14;
int intPi = (int) pi; // 3
```

> **Wrapper Caching:** `Integer.valueOf()` caches values from -128 to 127 to save memory (Flyweight Pattern).

subTopics.name: 1.4 Operators & Control Flow
1.5 Arrays
1.6 java.lang Core Classes
subTopics.description:
# The `java.lang` Package
Implicitly imported in every Java program.

## 1. String, StringBuilder, StringBuffer
- **String**: Immutable. Backed by `byte[]` (Java 9+). Uses String Pool.
- **StringBuilder**: Mutable, not synchronized (Fastest).
- **StringBuffer**: Mutable, synchronized (Thread-safe, slower).

```java
String s = "Java";
s = s.concat(" 17"); // Creates new object

StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("Java");
sb.append(" 17"); // Modifies in place
```

## 2. Object Class (Root Parent)
Key methods to override:
- `toString()`: Meaningful representation.
- `equals(Object)`: Deep content equality.
- `hashCode()`: Formula `31 * field + field`.
- `clone()`: Needs `Cloneable` interface.

## 3. Math & System
```java
Math.pow(2, 3); // 8.0
Math.random(); // 0.0 to 1.0

System.out.println(System.currentTimeMillis()); // Epoch time
System.arraycopy(src, 0, dest, 0, length); // Fast native copy
```

name: PHASE 2: OBJECT-ORIENTED PROGRAMMING
description:
# Phase 2: Object-Oriented Programming (OOP)

Java is strictly object-oriented. This phase covers classes, the 4 pillars of OOP, interfaces, and advanced class structures like Enums and Records.

subTopics.name: 2.1 Classes & Objects
2.2 The Four Pillars of OOP
2.3 Abstract Classes vs Interfaces
subTopics.description:
# Abstract Classes vs Interfaces

## Abstract Class
- Keyword: `abstract`
- Can have state (instance variables) and constructors.
- Can have both abstract (no body) and concrete methods.
- A class can extend only ONE abstract class.

## Interface
- Keyword: `interface`
- A contract indicating capabilities (e.g., `Runnable`).
- A class can implement MULTIPLE interfaces.
- **Java 8+**: Can have `default` and `static` methods with bodies.
- **Java 9+**: Can have `private` methods.

```java
interface Flyable {
    void fly(); // implicitly public abstract
    
    default void land() {
        System.out.println("Landing");
    }
}
```

subTopics.name: 2.4 Advanced Class Features

name: PHASE 3: COLLECTIONS FRAMEWORK
description:
# Phase 3: Collections Framework

The Java Collections Framework (JCF) provides data structures and algorithms to store and manipulate groups of data.

subTopics.name: 3.1 List (Ordered, Duplicates)
subTopics.description:
# List Interface
Ordered collections that allow duplicates.

## `ArrayList<E>`
- **Implementation**: Resizable Array.
- **Growth**: 50% when full.
- **Time Complexity**: `get()` is O(1), `add()` is amortized O(1), `insert/remove` in middle is O(n).
- **Use Case**: Frequent retrieval, rare middle insertions.

## `LinkedList<E>`
- **Implementation**: Doubly-Linked List.
- **Time Complexity**: `get()` is O(n), `addFirst/Last` is O(1).
- **Use Case**: Stacks/Queues, frequent insertions at ends.

> **Fail-fast Iterator**: If a list is structurally modified while iterating (except via the iterator's own remove method), a `ConcurrentModificationException` is thrown.

subTopics.name: 3.2 Set (No Duplicates)
subTopics.description:
# Set Interface
Collections that guarantee uniqueness. Rely heavily on `equals()` and `hashCode()`.

## `HashSet<E>`
- Backed by a HashMap.
- **Time Complexity**: O(1) average for basic operations.
- **Ordering**: None.

## `LinkedHashSet<E>`
- HashSet + Doubly-Linked List.
- Maintains **insertion order**.

## `TreeSet<E>`
- Backed by a Red-Black Tree (TreeMap).
- **Time Complexity**: O(log n).
- **Ordering**: Sorted automatically (Natural order or via `Comparator`).
- Implements `NavigableSet` (`ceiling`, `floor`, `higher`, `lower`).

subTopics.name: 3.3 Map (Key-Value Pairs)
subTopics.description:
# Map Interface
Not technically a `Collection`, but part of the framework. Unique keys mapped to values.

## `HashMap<K,V>`
- Uses hashing. Bucket index: `(n - 1) & hash`.
- Java 8+ converts buckets with >8 items from a LinkedList to a Red-Black Tree for performance (O(log n) worst case instead of O(n)).
- Allows ONE null key.

## `LinkedHashMap<K,V>`
- Maintains insertion order. 
- Can be configured for **Access Order** (useful for building LRU Caches).

## `TreeMap<K,V>`
- Red-Black tree. Keys are sorted.
- `O(log n)` operations.
- Implements `NavigableMap`.

> **Hashtable**: Legacy class. Thread-safe but slow. Does not allow nulls. Avoid in modern Java; use `ConcurrentHashMap` instead.

subTopics.name: 3.4 Queue, Deque & Utilities
subTopics.description:
# Queue, Deque & Utilities

## Queue & Deque
- **`PriorityQueue<E>`**: Min-Heap (or Max-Heap with custom comparator). O(log n) enqueue/dequeue.
- **`ArrayDeque<E>`**: Faster than LinkedList for Stack and Queue operations. Does not allow nulls.

## Sorting & Iteration
- **`Comparable<T>`**: Internal natural order. Must implement `int compareTo(T o)`.
- **`Comparator<T>`**: External custom order. Functional interface `int compare(T o1, T o2)`.

## Generics Wildcards
- `<T>`: Type parameter.
- `? extends T`: Upper bounded (Read-only collections, PECS - Producer Extends).
- `? super T`: Lower bounded (Write-only collections, PECS - Consumer Super).
- Type Erasure: JVM erases generic types at runtime for backward compatibility.

name: PHASE 4: EXCEPTIONS, I/O & NIO
description:
# Phase 4: Exceptions & Input/Output

Handling errors gracefully and interacting with the file system and network streams.

subTopics.name: 4.1 Exception Handling
subTopics.description:
# Exception Hierarchy

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Throwable] --> B[Error]
    A --> C[Exception]
    C --> D[Checked Exceptions]
    C --> E[RuntimeException]
```

- **`Error`**: Irrecoverable (e.g., `OutOfMemoryError`).
- **Checked Exceptions**: Compile-time enforcement (e.g., `IOException`). Must be caught or declared in `throws`.
- **Unchecked Exceptions**: Runtime (e.g., `NullPointerException`).

## `try-with-resources`
Java 7+ feature that auto-closes resources implementing `AutoCloseable`.
```java
try (BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new FileReader("file.txt"))) {
    System.out.println(br.readLine());
} // br is automatically closed here
```

## Custom Exceptions
- Extend `Exception` for Checked.
- Extend `RuntimeException` for Unchecked.

subTopics.name: 4.2 I/O Streams (java.io)
subTopics.description:
# I/O Streams

## Byte Streams (Binary Data)
Operates on 8-bit bytes.
- `InputStream` -> `FileInputStream`, `BufferedInputStream`
- `OutputStream` -> `FileOutputStream`, `BufferedOutputStream`

## Character Streams (Text Data)
Operates on 16-bit Unicode characters.
- `Reader` -> `FileReader`, `BufferedReader` (provides `readLine()`)
- `Writer` -> `FileWriter`, `BufferedWriter` (provides `newLine()`)

## Object Serialization
Converting object state to a byte stream.
- Requires marking class with `Serializable` interface.
- `ObjectOutputStream.writeObject()` and `ObjectInputStream.readObject()`.
- **`transient` keyword**: Skips a field during serialization.
- **`serialVersionUID`**: Ensures class version matches during deserialization.

subTopics.name: 4.3 NIO (java.nio)
subTopics.description:
# NIO (New I/O)
Introduced in Java 7 as NIO.2 to modernize file handling.

## `Path` and `Paths`
Modern replacement for `java.io.File`.

## `Files` Utility Class
Provides massive convenience methods:
```java
Path p = Path.of("data.txt");
List<String> lines = Files.readAllLines(p);
Files.writeString(p, "Hello World");
Files.copy(src, dest);
```

## Buffers & Channels
NIO is non-blocking and buffer-oriented.
- `ByteBuffer`: Direct buffers for fast native I/O.
- `FileChannel`: High performance, supports `transferTo()` for zero-copy OS-level transfers.
- `AsynchronousFileChannel`: For non-blocking I/O using Futures or Callbacks.

name: PHASE 5: CONCURRENCY & MULTITHREADING
description:
# Phase 5: Concurrency & Multithreading

Java provides deep support for concurrent programming, from low-level threads to high-level Executor frameworks.

subTopics.name: 5.1 Thread Fundamentals
subTopics.description:
# Thread Fundamentals
A Process has its own memory. A Thread shares the process Heap but has its own Stack.

## Creating Threads
1. **Extend `Thread` class** -> override `run()`.
2. **Implement `Runnable`** -> preferred, prevents subclassing lock-in.
3. **Implement `Callable<V>`** -> returns a value, can throw checked exceptions.

## Thread States
- `NEW`, `RUNNABLE`, `BLOCKED`, `WAITING`, `TIMED_WAITING`, `TERMINATED`.

## Key Methods
- `start()`: Fires up the JVM thread.
- `sleep(ms)`: Pauses execution.
- `join()`: Current thread waits for target thread to finish.
- `interrupt()`: Sets the interrupt flag on a thread.

subTopics.name: 5.2 Synchronization & Visibility
subTopics.description:
# Synchronization & Visibility

## `synchronized`
Intrinsic locks. Can be applied to methods (locks `this` or `Class.class`) or blocks (locks specific object).

## `volatile`
Guarantees **visibility** (flushes writes to main memory, bypassing CPU cache) and prevents instruction reordering. Does NOT guarantee atomicity.

## Inter-Thread Communication
Must be called from within a synchronized block:
- `wait()`: Releases lock, thread enters WAITING state.
- `notify()` / `notifyAll()`: Wakes up waiting thread(s).

## `java.util.concurrent.locks.Lock`
- `ReentrantLock`: More flexible than synchronized (tryLock, interruptible).
- `ReadWriteLock`: Multiple readers, single writer.

subTopics.name: 5.3 High-Level Concurrency
subTopics.description:
# High-Level Concurrency (`java.util.concurrent`)

## Executor Framework (Thread Pools)
Manages thread lifecycles.
```java
ExecutorService pool = Executors.newFixedThreadPool(4);
Future<Integer> future = pool.submit(() -> { return 42; });
pool.shutdown();
```

## Concurrent Collections
- `ConcurrentHashMap`: Lock-striping and CAS (Compare-And-Swap) for high throughput.
- `CopyOnWriteArrayList`: Read-heavy, writes clone the array.
- `BlockingQueue`: Bounded queues that block on empty/full (used in Producer/Consumer).

## Synchronizers
- **`CountDownLatch`**: Threads wait for a counter to hit zero.
- **`CyclicBarrier`**: N threads wait for each other to reach a point.
- **`Semaphore`**: Restricts concurrent access to limited resources.

## `CompletableFuture` (Java 8)
Asynchronous, reactive programming.
```java
CompletableFuture.supplyAsync(() -> "Data")
    .thenApply(String::toUpperCase)
    .thenAccept(System.out::println);
```

name: PHASE 6: MODERN JAVA (Java 8+ Features)
description:
# Phase 6: Modern Java

Java 8 revolutionized the language with functional paradigms. Later versions added further syntactical sugar.

subTopics.name: 6.1 Lambdas & Functional Interfaces
subTopics.description:
# Lambdas & Functional Interfaces

## Lambdas
Concise implementation of a functional interface.
` (parameters) -> { body } `
- **Variable Capture**: Can access `final` or `effectively final` local variables.

## Core `@FunctionalInterface`s
- **`Predicate<T>`**: `boolean test(T)` -> Filtering
- **`Consumer<T>`**: `void accept(T)` -> Processing (forEach)
- **`Function<T,R>`**: `R apply(T)` -> Mapping/Transformation
- **`Supplier<T>`**: `T get()` -> Lazy Generation

## Method References
Shorthand for lambdas:
- `System.out::println` instead of `s -> System.out.println(s)`
- `String::length` instead of `s -> s.length()`
- `ArrayList::new` instead of `() -> new ArrayList<>()`

subTopics.name: 6.2 Streams API
subTopics.description:
# Streams API (`java.util.stream`)
Declarative pipeline processing for collections.

## Pipeline Architecture
1. **Source** (e.g., `list.stream()`)
2. **Intermediate Ops** (Lazy): `filter`, `map`, `flatMap`, `sorted`, `distinct`, `limit`.
3. **Terminal Ops** (Eager): `forEach`, `collect`, `reduce`, `count`, `anyMatch`, `findFirst`.

## Example
```java
List<String> names = List.of("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie");
String result = names.stream()
    .filter(n -> n.length() > 3)
    .map(String::toUpperCase)
    .collect(Collectors.joining(", ")); // "ALICE, CHARLIE"
```

## Parallel Streams
`list.parallelStream()` uses the common `ForkJoinPool` to process chunks concurrently.

subTopics.name: 6.3 Optional & Date/Time API
subTopics.description:
# Optional & Date/Time API

## `Optional<T>`
Prevents `NullPointerException` by wrapping nullable values.
```java
Optional<User> opt = userRepository.findById(1);
opt.ifPresent(u -> System.out.println(u.getName()));
User u = opt.orElseThrow(() -> new NotFoundException());
```

## `java.time` API
Immutable, thread-safe date/time handling.
- **`LocalDate`**, **`LocalTime`**, **`LocalDateTime`**: No timezone.
- **`ZonedDateTime`**: With timezone.
- **`Instant`**: Machine time (epoch seconds/nanos).
- **`Duration`** (time-based) & **`Period`** (date-based) to calculate differences.
- **`DateTimeFormatter`** for parsing/formatting.

name: PHASE 7: JVM INTERNALS (Tuning & Diagnostics)
description:
# Phase 7: JVM Internals

Understanding the JVM architecture is critical for performance tuning and resolving memory leaks.

subTopics.name: 7.1 JVM Memory Layout
subTopics.description:
# JVM Runtime Data Areas

## Shared Memory (All Threads)
- **Heap**: Stores instantiated objects. Divided into Young Gen (Eden, S0, S1) and Old Gen (Tenured).
- **Metaspace**: Stores class metadata, statics, and JIT compiled code. (Replaced PermGen in Java 8).

## Thread-Local Memory
- **Stack**: Stores Frames. A Frame is created for every method call. Contains Local Variables, Operand Stack.
- **PC Register**: Points to the current bytecode instruction.

```mermaid
pie title Memory Distribution
    "Heap (Objects)" : 70
    "Metaspace (Classes)" : 15
    "Stacks (Threads)" : 10
    "Native/Other" : 5
```

subTopics.name: 7.2 Garbage Collection
subTopics.description:
# Garbage Collection

Automatic memory management identifying unreferenced objects using **GC Roots** (Active threads, local vars, statics).

## Algorithms
- **Mark-Copy**: Used in Young Gen. Surviving objects are copied between survivor spaces.
- **Mark-Compact**: Used in Old Gen. Compacts memory to prevent fragmentation.

## GC Implementations
- **Serial GC**: Single thread. Small apps.
- **Parallel GC**: Throughput optimized.
- **G1GC (Garbage-First)**: Default since Java 9. Predictable pause times.
- **ZGC**: Ultra-low latency (<1ms pause times) for massive heaps.

## Critical JVM Flags
- `-Xms`: Initial heap size.
- `-Xmx`: Maximum heap size.
- `-XX:+HeapDumpOnOutOfMemoryError`: Crucial for post-mortem leak analysis.

subTopics.name: 7.3 Class Loading Mechanism
subTopics.description:
# Class Loading

Classes are loaded dynamically via the **Parent Delegation Model**.

## Lifecycle Phases
1. **Loading**: Read `.class` bytes.
2. **Linking**: Verification (security), Preparation (statics get default values), Resolution (direct references).
3. **Initialization**: Static blocks and assignments execute.

## Classloader Hierarchy
1. **Bootstrap Classloader**: Native code, loads core `java.*` classes.
2. **Platform Classloader**: Loads extension libraries.
3. **Application Classloader**: Loads your CLASSPATH classes.

> *Custom Classloaders* are used by servers like Tomcat to isolate web applications.
